_note: Watch list for the Phase 2 proposal engine. The weekly scheduled agent iterates these, fetches upcoming events, applies the taste filter (see chriso.org memory: project_calendar_taste.md), diffs against seen.json, enriches candidates, and emails Christopher a digest. Add new sources by appending to this array; flag items that need human input with status: 'needs-*'.

_schema.name: display name
_schema.ical_url: iCal/ICS feed if discoverable (else null — agent will scrape)
_schema.filter_notes: per-source guidance the agent should apply when filtering

sources.name: Vashon Center for the Arts
sources.filter_notes: On-island; HIGH BAR for the mainstream acoustic-folk bookings the venue tends toward. Surface only genuinely unexpected programming — a string quartet playing Shostakovich here WOULD make the cut, a mainstream folk-acoustic night would NOT. 90%+ under-18 free (4Culture).

sources.name: Open Space for Arts & Community (O Space)
sources.filter_notes: On-island multi-use venue — music, performance, physical arts (UMO School), community. Low bar for inclusion; most programming is interesting-by-default. No iCal feed — agent must scrape the /events page each run.

sources.name: The Coup
sources.filter_notes: On-island DIY venue. Same low bar as O Space.
sources.notes: Christopher confirmed as a source 2026-04-23. Web search 2026-04-23 did NOT find a venue by this name — possibly private/invite-only, a newer space, or the name is slightly different. Christopher to clarify (nickname? correct spelling?).

sources.name: Salish Sea Early Music Festival — Vashon Island
sources.filter_notes: Renaissance + early baroque repertoire on period instruments. Vashon concerts are Mondays at 12:00 noon, typically at Bethel Church. Suggested donation $15/$20/$25; free under 18. Generally passes — consistent taste match.
sources.notes: No iCal feed. Email list: salishseafestival@aol.com — consider subscribing claude@chriso.org (once that exists) for announcements. Known 2026 Vashon dates: Jan 19, Feb 16, Feb 23, Mar 23, Apr 27, May 11, Jun 8, Jun 29.

sources.name: Martin Koenig — Balkan / folk traditions, Vashon
sources.filter_notes: NOT A VENUE — Martin Koenig is the Vashon ethnomusicologist/folk-dance specialist Christopher mentioned. His 1968 recording of 'Izlel e Delyu' (sung by Valya Balkanska) is on the Voyager Golden Record. Founder of Balkan Arts Center → Center for Traditional Music and Dance. Christopher wants anything Martin produces/promotes to be surfaced. Ingestion channel still TBD — Christopher said he doesn't know how Martin advertises his events.
sources.notes: Identity confirmed via web research 2026-04-23. Ingestion mechanism pending: possibilities include (a) checking East European Folklife Center listings / Jack Straw Cultural Center artist page, (b) watching for Vashon Beachcomber mentions, (c) Christopher asking Martin to forward announcements to a future claude@chriso.org address. Agent should web-search 'Martin Koenig' + 'Vashon' + upcoming events each run.

sources.name: Earshot Jazz
sources.filter_notes: Jazz focus — presents shows year-round + annual fall festival. Prefer adventurous programming, NIME-adjacent, improvisation-heavy, composer-led. Straight-ahead standards sets only if by a marquee player.

sources.name: The Crocodile
sources.filter_notes: Indie rock / heavy / experimental shows welcome. Skip generic pop bookings.

sources.name: Neumos
sources.filter_notes: Similar bar to The Crocodile.

sources.name: The Showbox
sources.filter_notes: Larger room — occasionally books the interesting stuff (Tomahawk/Melvins). Skip mainstream pop.

sources.name: Chapel Performance Space — Wayward Music Series
sources.filter_notes: Experimental / contemporary classical / improvised. Almost everything here passes the taste filter by default.

sources.name: Seattle Symphony
sources.filter_notes: SELECTIVE — apply mainstream-presenter calibration from taste-filter memory. PASS: Zappa, Messiaen, video-game-music, Miyazaki (barely), William Shatner performances, contemporary / experimental / multimedia programs. DOES NOT PASS: Harry Potter, Lord of the Rings, Beatles songbook, folk-pop crossovers. EXCEPTION: unusual framing (e.g. live score to a Beatles film) may flip a would-be reject.

sources.name: Velocity Dance Center
sources.filter_notes: Contemporary dance + improvisation. Good default.

sources.name: On the Boards
sources.filter_notes: Experimental performance — music, dance, theater. Good default.

sources.name: The Royal Room (Wayne Horvitz's club)
sources.filter_notes: Wayne Horvitz's venue — jazz, improv, experimental. Most shows pass.

sources.name: Freehold Theater
sources.filter_notes: Theater — experimental / movement-based only (per genre scope; no straight plays). Verify still has a performance space running.
sources.notes: Christopher flagged 'if that still exists'; confirm before activating.

sources.name: Meany Hall / UW World Series
sources.filter_notes: UW's flagship performing-arts series — dance, world music, chamber, experimental. Usually passes the filter; they curate at a high bar.

sources.name: Cornish College of the Arts (now at Seattle University)
sources.filter_notes: Cornish merged into Seattle University in 2025 — events now appear on BOTH the cornish.edu calendar AND events.seattleu.edu. Historically Cornish ran free noon-concert and student/faculty recital series with lots of challenging work. Status of the legacy noon-concert series is UNCLEAR after the merger — Christopher asked to verify. Contact: tickets@cornish.edu. Agent should scrape both URLs each run and flag anything that looks noon-concert-series-adjacent.
sources.notes: Christopher questioned 'primary feed?' framing — the noon-concert status is uncertain post-merger. Treat as a normal source until we verify the noon-concert pipeline is intact.

sources.name: UW DXARTS
sources.filter_notes: Digital Arts and Experimental Media at UW — electronic/computer-music performances, sound art, experimental media. Almost all passes.

sources.name: Centrum (Port Townsend) — Jazz, Chamber, Voiceworks
sources.filter_notes: Festival + workshops programming at Fort Worden. The Jazz Port Townsend festival (late July) is a flagship event. Dawn Clement is the new AD (succeeded John Clayton).

sources.name: Substation
sources.filter_notes: Small Ballard venue — heavy, experimental, metal, punk, weird rock. Most bookings pass the taste filter. Confirmed source 2026-04-24 (Sleepytime Gorilla Museum 5/30 show on their calendar).

artist_watches.name: Rinde Eckert
artist_watches.notes: Avant-garde theater + music. Watch for any performance featuring him — any venue, any city within reasonable travel.

artist_watches.name: Meredith Monk
artist_watches.notes: Watch for any performance featuring her or her ensemble — any venue.

artist_watches.name: Gamelan Pacifica
artist_watches.notes: Resident at Cornish. Christopher noted they may have a 'resource about new albums' that often includes performance listings — check their site/blog/news during each run. All Gamelan Pacifica performances pass by default.

artist_watches.name: Significant contemporary choreographers
artist_watches.notes: Watch for touring work by living choreographers doing challenging modern dance — examples: Ohad Naharin, William Forsythe, Crystal Pite, Akram Khan, Ralph Lemon, Trisha Brown Dance Company, Mark Morris Dance Group, Stephen Petronio, Kyle Abraham / A.I.M, Faye Driscoll, etc. Not exhaustive — use taste. Traditional classical ballet does NOT pass unless the choreography is the draw.

artist_watches.name: Bandsintown / Spotify concert suggestions
artist_watches.notes: Christopher uses Bandsintown and Spotify's concert suggestions (based on his listening history). Hit rate is mixed — 'often gives me great suggestions (and often not)'. Mechanism for getting these into the weekly proposal flow is TBD — ideas: (a) Christopher forwards notable suggestions to a claude@chriso.org once that exists, (b) Bandsintown has a public API (https://app.bandsintown.com/docs) — if Christopher gives us a list of 'artists I follow', the agent can poll that directly, (c) the agent can crawl Bandsintown's public artist pages for the artists in our artist_watches[] list. Filter notes: apply the normal taste bar; many Spotify/Bandsintown suggestions will be mainstream pop that doesn't pass.

artist_watches.name: Martin Koenig — recommendations
artist_watches.notes: Identity confirmed 2026-04-23 via web research. See sources entry martin-koenig-recs for details. Ingestion mechanism still TBD.
